Israeli forces kill Palestinian teenager in West Bank village near Nablus
articles.summary: Israeli forces kill Palestinian teenager in West Bank village near Nablus Israeli forces shot and killed a Palestinian teenager late last night in the village of Al-Lubban ash-Sharqiya, south of Nablus in the occupied West Bank, Wafa news agency reported. Local sources said the forces shot 16-year-old Fahd Zidan Owais while he was in an open area of the village. The sources added that Israeli forces withheld the teenager’s body and prevented the Palestine Red Crescent Society crews from reaching him.

articles.title: Morning update
articles.summary: Morning update Good morning Middle East Eye readers, Israel launched another series of attacks against towns across Lebanon, especially targeting south of the country, in violation of a ceasefire agreement in mid-April.  The shelling and strikes came in spite of peace talks between Tel Aviv and Beirut, with one senior US State Department official commenting that the two countries enjoyed a "full day of productive and positive talks” on Thursday. Here are some of the other latest developments in the region:  US President Donald Trump said he does not have much patience left with Iran,

articles.title: Israel issues forced displacement orders for five towns, villages in south Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel issues forced displacement orders for five towns, villages in south Lebanon Israel’s military has issued a forced evacuation order for the residents of five towns and villages amid its ongoing war on Lebanon. In a post on social media, the military listed the targeted areas in the south of the country as Shabriha, Hamideh, Zuqaq al-Mufdi, Mashouk, and El Haouch. Residents have been warned to “evacuate” immediately to open areas at least 1,000 metres from their homes. Smoke rises from the site of an Israeli airstrike that targeted the southern Lebanese village of Jarjouaa on 13 May 2026.

articles.title: Hezbollah claims targeting of Israeli drones with missiles
articles.summary: Hezbollah claims targeting of Israeli drones with missiles Hezbollah announced in a series of statements that it targeted Israeli aircrafts and drones in the skies over southern Lebanon with surface-to-air missiles. The group also claimed attacks on Israeli forces in several areas south of the country.  The statements came amid reports of sirens activating in northern Israel, following what Israeli reports described as rocket fire from Lebanon at dawn.

articles.title: Israeli shelling reported in Lebanon, as army issues expulsion orders to towns
articles.summary: Israeli shelling reported in Lebanon, as army issues expulsion orders to towns The Israeli army early on Friday issued expulsion threats to residents in several towns, a number of them in or near the southern city of Tyre.  In a post on social media, the military listed the targeted areas in the south of the country as Shabriha, Hamideh, Zuqaq al-Mufdi, Mashouk, and El Haouch. Meanwhile, artillery shelling and shooting was reported in a few areas south of the country, including the town of Qalawiya and Wadi al-Hujeir. An overnight drone attack also targeted an apartmen

articles.title: Israel says soldier killed in south Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel says soldier killed in south Lebanon Israel's military said Friday that one of its soldiers died in combat in southern Lebanon, bringing its losses to 20 personnel since the war on Lebanon began in early March. Staff Sergeant Negev Dagan, 20, died "during combat in southern Lebanon", the military said, without providing additional information.

articles.title: Trump says China's Xi has become a 'friend'
articles.summary: Trump says China's Xi has become a 'friend' US President Donald Trump says his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping has "become really a friend", asserting that the two leaders "settled a lot of different problems that other people wouldn’t have been able to settle". Trump also expressed he made "fantastic trade deals" with China. On the war on Iran, the US president indicated that both leaders "feel very similar", saying: "We want that to end. We we don’t want them to have a nuclear weapon. We want the straits open."

articles.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian following settler attack
articles.summary: Israeli forces kill Palestinian following settler attack One Palestinian was killed and another wounded by Israeli forces overnight in a town south of Nablus, occupied West Bank, following an attack by settlers in the same area.  Late on Thursday, settlers stormed the area and attempted to attack residents' properties. Israeli forces later raided the town, firing live ammunition and sound bombs at the residents.

articles.title: Israel to sue New York Times over article detailing rape of Palestinians detainees
articles.summary: Israel to sue New York Times over article detailing rape of Palestinians detainees Israel is preparing legal action against The New York Times after the newspaper published an article describing the rapes of Palestinian prisoners in its custody. The Israeli Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) announced its decision to sue on X on Thursday, after the paper doubled down in its defence of the report. "Nicholas Kristof’s deeply reported piece of opinion journalism starts with a proposition to readers: ‘Whatever our views of the Middle East conflict, we should be able to unite in condemning rape,'" a

articles.title: Report: Israel and US weighing options for renewed attacks on Iran
articles.summary: Report: Israel and US weighing options for renewed attacks on Iran Israeli and US military officials held meetings on plans to take in the case of renewed military action against Iran over the past week, according to a report by the Israeli public broadcaster Kan. The report indicated that senior Israeli military officials and representatives from US Central Command discussed scenarios involving US strikes aimed at pressuring Tehran to return to negotiations as well as tightening a maritime blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Tel Aviv also reportedly expressed to Washing

articles.title: China calls for peace in the Middle East, urges reopening of shipping lanes
articles.summary: China calls for peace in the Middle East, urges reopening of shipping lanes China on Friday stressed that a ceasefire must be reached urgently in the region, with trade routes reopened "as soon as possible". "A comprehensive and lasting ceasefire should be reached as soon as possible to facilitate the early restoration of peace and stability in the Middle East and the Gulf region," Beijing's foreign ministry said in a statement. "Shipping lanes should be reopened as soon as possible in response to the calls of the international community," it added.

articles.title: Settlers set ablaze vehicles, spray racist slogans in Ramallah
articles.summary: Settlers set ablaze vehicles, spray racist slogans in Ramallah A group of settlers early on Friday set fire to a number of vehicles in a village in Ramallah, occupied West Bank, the latest attack in a wave of escalating Israeli violence. Local residents in the village of Jibya reported that settlers infiltrated the outskirts of the village, torching several vehicles, with the subsequent fire causing material damage to their surrounding. The settlers had also vandalised walls of the village and residents' properties with racist slogans. A group of settlers torched vehicles in the vill
